semanticCore: Gushing, bubbling spring, flowing water, source, or arrow wood
quranicRelevance: Spring gushing from ground (Yanbu'a - 'tafjura lana... yanboo'an'), and Paradise springs ('yanabee'a').

keyDerivations.meaning: Spring, fountain, gushing water source, aquifer, or origin

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَسَلَكَهُ يَنَابِيعَ فِي الْأَرْضِ (39:21): Do you not see that God sends down rain from the sky and guides it into springs/aquifers in the earth?
discardedNoise: Arrow wood trees (Naba') in historic weaponry manuals.
News report bulletins (Naba') - spelling shift (belongs to n-b-a with hamza).
summaryEn: The root represents gushing water sources (Yanbu'), detailing the hydrology of rain-fed underground aquifers/springs and the Meccan demands for miraculous spring production.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر خروج الماء من الأرض ونبعه متدفقاً (نبع)، واصفاً شبكات ينابيع المياه الجوفية الأرضية، وطلب المشركين تفجير ينبوع بمكة.
rawText: أَنْبَعَ 4 He (God) made, or caused, water to issue. (Msb.) انْبَعُ , irregularly formed from the augmented verb أَنْبَعَ: see an ex. in a couplet cited voce سَقَى. نَبَعَ 1 It (water) welled, or issued forth. === نَبَعَ عَلَيْهِمْ: see نَبَأَ عليهم. نَبْعٌ The tree so-called: see an ex. of its n. un. in a verse cited voce تَحَوَّفَ and تَخَوَّفَ. === نَبْعٌ: see شَوْحَطٌ and شِرْيَانٌ and فَتْحٌ. === نَبْعَانِ The two shafts of a cart: so called because they were commonly made of wood of the tree called نَبْع: see رَادَّةٌ.
